context: Tests whether the agent implements a correct UGC rights management system including token generation, rights request messaging, status transitions, scope definition, and incentivized thank-you flow when rights are granted.

checklist.name: Status: rights-requested
checklist.description: Submission status is updated to 'rights-requested' (not just a generic pending/contacted state) after the rights request is sent

checklist.name: Secure token generated
checklist.description: A unique token (rightsToken or equivalent) is generated and stored with the submission to enable the rights grant link

checklist.name: Reply YES simplicity
checklist.description: The rights request message instructs the user to reply with a single word (e.g. 'YES') rather than directing them only to a form or complex process

checklist.name: #ugcpermission hashtag
checklist.description: The rights request comment/DM includes the '#ugcpermission' hashtag

checklist.name: Manage preferences link
checklist.description: The rights request message includes a link where the user can manage their preferences (a rights grant URL with the token)

checklist.name: Status: rights-granted
checklist.description: Submission status is updated to 'rights-granted' (exact value) when the user approves

checklist.name: Rights scope definition
checklist.description: Rights scope is recorded as covering website, ads, and email (all three) upon grant

checklist.name: Thank-you discount code
checklist.description: A 10% discount code is sent to the author via email as a thank-you after rights are granted

checklist.name: No publish without rights
checklist.description: The code that retrieves or displays UGC filters by status === 'rights-granted' and does NOT return/render content with any other status

checklist.name: rightsRequestedAt timestamp
checklist.description: A timestamp (rightsRequestedAt or equivalent) is recorded when the rights request is sent

checklist.name: rightsGrantedAt timestamp
checklist.description: A timestamp (rightsGrantedAt or equivalent) is recorded when rights are granted
